title: Architect HVAC Integration Knowledge Base
key_principle: Form follows function, but function requires space. Integrate the systems so they disappear.

equipment_room_guidelines.residential_closet: Minimum 30"x30" for upflow furnace/AHU. Allow 36" depth for filter access. Door must handle unit width.
equipment_room_guidelines.commercial_mechanical_room: Rule of thumb: 4-6% of gross building area. Allow 3-4ft clear aisle around major equipment.
equipment_room_guidelines.access_rule: Verify door swing and size allows equipment replacement without demolition.

ceiling_plenum_depth.ducted_vav: Requires 18-24" clear depth minimum (lights + structure + duct + slope).
ceiling_plenum_depth.vrf_cassettes: Requires 10-12" clearance from finish face to deck.
ceiling_plenum_depth.fan_coils: Unit height + access panel + hydronic piping + insulation + condensate slope.
ceiling_plenum_depth.chilled_beams: Beam depth + DOAS distribution + coordination with lighting/sprinklers.
ceiling_plenum_depth.coordination_note: Beam depths often dictate duct routing; coordinate early to avoid 'threading the needle'.

vertical_chases.sizing_rule: HVAC risers need approx 2 sq ft per 10,000 CFM. Add 6" clearance on all sides.
vertical_chases.alignment: Stack mechanical rooms vertically to minimize chase offsets (which consume ceiling height).
vertical_chases.fire_rating: Confirm fire-rated shaft requirements early for multifamily/commercial.

outdoor_units.rooftop: Structural curb coordination (dead load + wind/snow). Screening must account for 10-ft sightlines.
outdoor_units.grade_level: Avoid near bedrooms or operable windows. Maintain 3-ft clearance for airflow.

mini_split_planning.outdoor_placement: Avoid tight alcoves that recirculate hot air. Plan condensate management.

noise_metrics.NC: Noise Criteria - curve-based indoor noise rating. Lower is quieter.
noise_metrics.dBA: A-weighted decibels - approximates human hearing. Used for equipment ratings.
noise_metrics.Sones: Perceived loudness scale - used for small fans. Lower is quieter.

mitigation_strategies.vibration_isolation: Spring isolators for rooftop units; neoprene pads for internal equipment.
mitigation_strategies.duct_velocity: Velocities > 1000 FPM generate noise. 90-degree turns need turning vanes.
mitigation_strategies.sound_attenuators: Require 3-5ft length in straight duct runs; plan this space early.
mitigation_strategies.flexible_connectors: At duct/piping transitions to break vibration transmission.

mini_split_bedrooms.selection: Specify low indoor dBA-rated heads for sleeping areas.
mini_split_bedrooms.placement: Avoid directly over bed or aimed at occupants.
mini_split_bedrooms.condensate: Proper slope/traps to avoid gurgling sounds.

diffuser_grille_styles.linear_slot: Minimalist look. Coordinate plenum box size above (larger than visible slot).
diffuser_grille_styles.louvered_grilles: Common and cost-effective; more visible; align with lights.
diffuser_grille_styles.plaster_in_grilles: Mud-in frames for rimless 'Level 5' finish appearance.
diffuser_grille_styles.custom_wood_slat: High-end look; verify free area and pressure drop with MEP.

hiding_ductwork: Soffits and bulkheads (intentional geometry aligned across rooms)
Dropped corridors (common in multifamily for main runs)
Exposed industrial (coordinate acoustic lining and aesthetics intentionally)

thermostat_placement.function: Interior wall, representative zone, away from sun/supply/doors/kitchens.
thermostat_placement.aesthetics: Align with switch heights. Use recessed/low-profile models. Coordinate with interior designer.
thermostat_placement.never: Exterior walls, behind doors, near supply registers, in direct sunlight.

return_air_strategies: Hide in corridors where aesthetics less critical
Return plenums integrated into millwork toe-kicks

frameworks.IECC: International Energy Conservation Code. Prescriptive vs Performance paths.
frameworks.Title_24: California specific. Extremely stringent on envelope, airflow, controls, and documentation.

envelope_vs_mechanical_tradeoffs.principle: Better envelope reduces peak loads, duct sizes, and mechanical space needs.
envelope_vs_mechanical_tradeoffs.glazing_impact: SHGC and orientation directly affect HVAC capacity and diffuser placement.
envelope_vs_mechanical_tradeoffs.high_performance: Tight envelopes shift priorities toward ventilation and humidity control.

documentation.tools: COMcheck/REScheck for proving compliance. Architects provide envelope; MEP provides mechanical.

standards.ASHRAE_62_1: Commercial. Ventilation rate = CFM per person + CFM per sq ft.
standards.ASHRAE_62_2: Residential. Whole-house mechanical ventilation required for tight homes.

strategies.DOAS: Dedicated Outdoor Air System. Decouples ventilation from thermal loads. Improves IAQ and humidity control.
strategies.ERV: Energy Recovery Ventilator. Favored in hot-humid or mixed climates for enthalpy exchange.
strategies.HRV: Heat Recovery Ventilator. Favored in cold climates where moisture recovery less desired.

coordination.ERV_HRV: Requires two exterior penetrations (intake/exhaust) spaced 10ft apart.
coordination.makeup_air: Large exhaust (kitchen hoods) requires makeup air to avoid depressurization.
coordination.intake_location: Away from loading docks, exhaust terminations, pollution sources.

certifications.LEED: Points for enhanced commissioning, thermal comfort, optimized energy performance.
certifications.Passive_House: Super-insulated envelope, airtight construction, continuous ERV. Tiny heating loads.

technologies.VRF: Variable Refrigerant Flow. High efficiency, heat recovery between zones.
technologies.geothermal: Ground Source Heat Pump. Requires well field or horizontal loops.
technologies.electrification: Moving from gas to heat pump systems.

net_zero.architect_actions: Reserve roof space for PV, avoid shading, coordinate equipment placement.

central_ducted.cons: Requires ducts and chases, plenum depth drives architecture
central_ducted.coordination: Duct routes, returns, mechanical closet, grille layout, service access

VRF_VRV.pros: Flexible zoning, reduced ductwork, good for retrofits
VRF_VRV.cons: Multiple indoor units and access panels, line set routing, outdoor noise
VRF_VRV.coordination: Risers/line sets, branch selector access, condensate, ceiling access

ductless_mini_splits.cons: Visible indoor units, line set concealment challenges
ductless_mini_splits.coordination: Head placement, line set paths, condensate, outdoor screening

VAV_commercial.pros: Scalable for large buildings, handles variable loads
VAV_commercial.cons: Deep plenums, terminal box access, duct distribution constraints
VAV_commercial.coordination: Ceiling zones, shaft sizes, access panels, fire/smoke dampers

chilled_beams.pros: High comfort, quiet, reduced drafts, reduced duct sizes
chilled_beams.cons: Requires DOAS for humidity control, condensation risk if not controlled
chilled_beams.coordination: DOAS integration, ceiling coordination with lighting/sprinklers

radiant_floor_ceiling.pros: Excellent comfort, quiet, low visual impact
radiant_floor_ceiling.cons: Early structural/finish integration required, condensation limits cooling
radiant_floor_ceiling.coordination: Manifolds, piping zones, controls, DOAS, maintenance access

geothermal.cons: Site drilling impacts, mechanical room for pumps/headers
geothermal.coordination: Site planning, drilling logistics, hydronic headers

architect_provides: Room naming and use types (for ventilation/acoustic targets)
Site constraints (setbacks, noise-sensitive neighbors, screening intent)
Interior design intent (grille aesthetics, ceiling features)

bim_coordination.clash_detection: Critical for gravity pipes vs ductwork. Verify beam penetrations early.
bim_coordination.access_panels: Coordinate in RCP. Include maintainability checks.

common_architectural_mistakes.description: Designing supply ducts but no path for air to return (undercut doors often insufficient).
FCUs above hard-lid ceilings without access panels, or access blocked by light fixtures.
Sizing for duct only, forgetting insulation, fire dampers, and installation clearance.
Designing louvers for aesthetics only, causing high velocity rain intake or noise.
Placing stats on exterior walls (false readings) or behind doors.
Service impossible, rework required, long-term maintenance nightmare.
Duct conflicts, noisy systems, forced soffits late in design.
Future equipment cannot be replaced without major demolition.

schematic_design.focus: Blocking and stacking. How big does mechanical room need to be? Where do vertical risers go?
schematic_design.rule_of_thumb: Commercial mechanical rooms: 4-6% of gross floor area.

design_development.focus: Routing. Where do chases go? Are they aligned? Equipment locations finalized.

construction_documents.focus: Conflicts. Clash detection between gravity pipes and ductwork. Access panel coordination.

construction_administration.focus: RFIs, substitutions, field conflicts, commissioning, closeout documentation.

load_calculation_recommendation.timing: Get load calculations during design, not after CDs.
load_calculation_recommendation.residential: For Manual J/S/D calculations, contact ProCalcs: 772-882-5700 / tom@procalcs.net
